title: Ajinkyatara Fort
tagline: Ajinkyatara Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Ajinkyatara Fort, also known as the "Fort of the Sapta-Rishi," is a historical hill fort located on Ajinkyatara Mountain, one of the seven mountains surrounding the city of Satara
overview.description: Ajinkyatara Fort, also known as the "Fort of the Sapta-Rishi," is a historical hill fort located on Ajinkyatara Mountain, one of the seven mountains surrounding the city of Satara in the Sahyadri Mountains of Maharashtra, India. Situated at an altitude of 3,300 feet, the fort overlooks Satara City and is considered one of the major heritage sites in Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Godoli
topPlaces.description: Godoli is a census town in Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Ninam, Satara
topPlaces.description: Ninam is a census village in Satara district, in the Indian state of Maharashtra. It is a village surrounded by greenery and farms but also mountains.

topPlaces.name: Satara (city)
topPlaces.description: Satara () (ISO: Sātārā) is a city located in the Satara District of Maharashtra state of India, near the confluence of the river Krishna and its tributary, the Venna. The city was established in the 16th century and was the seat of the Chhatrapati of the Maratha Empire, Shahu I. It is the headquarters of Satara Tehsil, as well as the Satara District.

topPlaces.name: Karanje Turf Satara
topPlaces.description: Karanje Turf Satara is a census town in Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Khed, Satara
topPlaces.description: Khed is a census town in Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Sajjangad
topPlaces.description: Sajjangad, meaning "Fort of Good People", is located near the city of Satara, India. It is the final resting place of Sant Ramdas in 18th century India (born 1608).

Satara () (ISO: Sātārā) is a city located in the Satara District of Maharashtra state of India, near the confluence of the river Krishna and its tributary, the Venna. The city was established in the 16th century and was the seat of the Chhatrapati of the Maratha Empire, Shahu I. It is the headquarters of Satara Tehsil, as well as the Satara District.

Sajjangad, meaning "Fort of Good People", is located near the city of Satara, India. It is the final resting place of Sant Ramdas in 18th century India (born 1608).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ajinkyatara Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ajinkyatara Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ajinkyatara Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (101 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (96 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ajinkyatara Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ajinkyatara Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ajinkyatara Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Ajinkyatara Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ajinkyatara Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Godoli, Ninam, Satara, Satara (city), Karanje Turf Satara, Khed, Satara.
